title: Nested try-catch-finally Flow
description: A flowchart depicting the execution flow and exception handling within nested try statements in Java.
relationships: Flow progresses from outer try to inner try. If an exception is thrown in the inner try, the JVM first looks for a matching catch block in the inner try's handlers. If none is found, the exception propagates to the outer try's catch handlers. finally blocks always execute for their respective try, regardless of how the block was exited.
